page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY physical cores operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing is relatively weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmarks. Memory bandwidth remains average, reaching 59.2 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 49.35 GB/sec for writes, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

Given its high memory density and average multi-core performance, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand extreme single-thread CPU speed. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis (achieving 8.48 million SET operations per second in testing), and moderate multi-threaded data processing. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks, high-throughput web serving, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs (x86_64 architecture) clocked at 2.5 GHz and 96.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU acceleration, it relies on a KVM hypervisor and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and bottom 10% for Geekbench, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth reach average tiers. This makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-dense, multi-threaded workloads. It is best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and moderate data processing tasks rather than single-threaded applications or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, offering average multi-core performance but weak single-core speeds.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring twelve dedicated cores and ninety-six gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive multi-threaded workloads.

96.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
KVM hypervisor with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for database operations and Redis caching
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
